data.description:

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Carpenter Tan Holdings Limited ( (HK:0837) ) has provided an update.

Carpenter Tan Holdings Limited has convened its 2025 annual general meeting for 22 May 2026 in Jurong City, Jiangsu Province, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and consider the re-election of retiring directors with board-authorised remuneration. The meeting will also vote on appointing Crowe (HK) CPA Limited as the new auditor, approving a final dividend of HK38.31 cents per share for eligible shareholders as of 8 June 2026, and granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s existing share capital, moves that collectively shape the company’s capital management flexibility, governance continuity and shareholder returns.

More about Carpenter Tan Holdings Limited

Carpenter Tan Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 837. The group operates in the consumer products sector, with its business conducted primarily in Mainland China, and maintains a governance structure overseen by a board of directors and external auditors in line with Hong Kong listing requirements.

data.title: Lingbao Gold Details Use of HK$770.6 Million Share Placement Proceeds for Overseas Gold Expansion
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Lingbao Gold Group Company Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3330) ) just unveiled an update.

Lingbao Gold Group has clarified how it will use the roughly HK$770.6 million in net proceeds from its recent top-up subscription of new H shares, earmarking the funds primarily for overseas expansion of its gold mining business and general working capital. The announcement underscores the group’s push to strengthen its global resource base and support ongoing operations.

Around 65% of the proceeds, or about HK$500.9 million, will fund expansion works at St Barbara Mining’s Simberi gold mine in Papua New Guinea, which the company acquired control of in April 2026, with completion and operation targeted for late 2027. A further 5%, or roughly HK$38.5 million, is allocated to future overseas mergers and acquisitions of sizeable, cash-generative gold projects by the end of 2026, reinforcing Lingbao Gold’s capacity to move quickly on cross-border deals and deepen its overseas footprint despite no specific new targets yet identified.

More about Lingbao Gold Group Company Ltd. Class H

Lingbao Gold Group Company Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated gold producer focused on exploration, mining, and development of gold resources, with its H shares listed in Hong Kong. The group has been advancing an internationalisation strategy, building a portfolio of overseas gold mining assets to secure long-term resource reserves and sustainable growth.

data.title: Dynagreen Reshapes Board as Veteran Executive Hu Shengyong Becomes Employee Director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1330) ) is now available.

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group announced that long-serving executive Mr. Hu Shengyong has resigned as an executive director and member of the nomination committee, following adjustments to the company’s corporate governance structure, with both sides confirming there is no disagreement. At the same time, Hu was elected by the employee representative meeting as the board’s employee director for the current term, a move that preserves his influence within the group while strengthening employee representation and leaving the board to plan his reappointment to the nomination committee without additional director’s fees.

Hu, a senior accountant who has held multiple key roles including CFO, board secretary and executive director since 2008, will continue to serve as deputy Party Committee secretary, trade union chairman and now employee director. His transition from executive to employee director suggests a reshaping rather than a reduction of his role in governance, maintaining continuity for stakeholders while aligning the board’s composition with PRC company law and listed-company governance guidelines.

More about Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Dynagreen Environmental Protection Group Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated environmental services company listed in Hong Kong that focuses on environmental protection projects and related operations. The group’s activities are supported by a governance structure that includes executive, non-executive, independent and employee directors representing management, shareholders and staff interests.

data.title: YH Entertainment Deepens Shenzhen Yiqi Ties in Five-Year Pop Toy Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from YH Entertainment Group ( (HK:2306) ).

YH Entertainment Group has formalised a long-term strategic cooperation between its subsidiaries Tianjin Yihua and Yuhua Tongxing and connected party Shenzhen Yiqi to develop and operate pop toy-related business. Under the agreements, effective from January 2025 to January 2030, Yuhua Tongxing will provide promotion services using the group’s managed artists, share economic benefits from sales of WAKUKU pop toys whose IP is owned by Shenzhen Yiqi, and sell pop toys based on its own IP to Shenzhen Yiqi and its affiliates.

In return, Yuhua Tongxing will pay Shenzhen Yiqi for consignment and marketing of pop toys, online marketing, IP design and production, and procurement of toys linked to Shenzhen Yiqi-owned or licensed IP, embedding Shenzhen Yiqi deeply in its value chain. As Yuhua Tongxing is no longer deemed an insignificant subsidiary, these arrangements now constitute continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering ongoing disclosure and annual review requirements and the appointment of an independent financial adviser to justify the five-year term as consistent with normal business practice.

More about YH Entertainment Group

YH Entertainment Group, listed in Hong Kong, operates through subsidiaries such as Tianjin Yihua and Yuhua Tongxing and is active in entertainment-related commercialisation, including artist management and licensing. The group is expanding into the pop toys segment, leveraging its managed artists and intellectual property to collaborate with consumer brands and IP owners in China.

Asia Television Holdings Limited ( (HK:0707) ) has shared an update.

Asia Television Holdings Limited has announced a series of senior corporate governance changes involving its company secretary and authorised representatives, reflecting an ongoing reshuffle of key compliance roles. Former company secretary and authorised representative Ho Yuk Ming Hugo, as well as successor Yu Tak Shing Eric and authorised representative Tang Po Yi, have all ceased their respective roles over the period from August 2025 to March 2026.

The board has appointed executive director Zha Mengling as an authorised representative from 13 April 2026 and named veteran solicitor Ling Wing Shan as both company secretary and authorised representative effective 17 April 2026. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3200) ) is now available.

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. has announced an updated final dividend for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, declaring a cash payout of RMB 6 per 10 shares to its H shareholders. The update specifies the timetable for shareholder approval on 8 May 2026, an ex-dividend date of 13 May 2026, the book closure from 15 to 18 May 2026, and confirms that the dividend will be paid in Hong Kong dollars with the exact amount and payment date to be announced.

The company also detailed the withholding tax arrangements for different categories of non-resident and mainland investors holding H shares, including a 10% enterprise income tax for non-resident enterprises and a 10% base rate for non-resident individuals, subject to applicable tax treaties. Mainland individual investors and certain funds accessing the stock via Shanghai-Hong Kong and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect will face a 20% withholding rate, clarifying after-tax entitlements and providing greater transparency for cross-border investors ahead of the distribution.

More about Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Han’s CNC Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based industrial company listed in Hong Kong, with its H shares traded under stock code 03200. The firm operates in the precision manufacturing and CNC equipment sector, serving industrial customers that rely on automated machining solutions in both mainland China and overseas markets.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2529) ) has provided an announcement.

Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group has agreed to acquire the entire equity interest of an unnamed target company for RMB49.54 million, turning it into a wholly owned subsidiary whose financial results will be consolidated into Hollwin’s accounts. The move expands Hollwin’s corporate structure and could strengthen its operational scale once completed.

Because the seller CSUDGCL is one of Hollwin’s controlling shareholders, the deal is classified in Hong Kong as both a discloseable and connected transaction, triggering additional reporting, circular and independent shareholder approval requirements. An extraordinary shareholders’ meeting will be convened, with an independent board committee and financial adviser appointed to opine on the fairness of the deal, and the company cautions investors that the acquisition may or may not proceed pending satisfaction of conditions.

More about Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. Class H

Hollwin Urban Operation Service Group Co., Ltd. is a mainland China-based urban operation service provider listed in Hong Kong, focusing on managing and operating urban-related assets and services. data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Kiu Hung International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0381) ) just unveiled an announcement.

AOM International Group is moving to scale its new wine business segment after acquiring Fujian Laojiu Investment Co., Ltd., a historic Fujian liquor brand with nearly two centuries of heritage and multiple national quality awards. The brand’s traditional brewing techniques are recognized as National Intangible Cultural Heritage, positioning the Group to blend cultural appeal with contemporary consumer trends in health-conscious dining.

The Group plans to launch Fujian Laojiu Stewed Pot self-operated restaurants and a franchise project in 2026, targeting the growing market for healthy, quality-focused dining amid economic headwinds and fierce competition in traditional restaurants. By investing tens of millions of yuan in a dedicated supply chain and using proprietary medicinal white mold and slow stewing methods, AOM aims to cut costs, enhance store profitability, and build a scalable chain brand spanning baijiu, cooking wines, condiments, beverages, and aged drinking wines.

More about Kiu Hung International Holdings Limited

AOM International Group Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company continued in Bermuda, operating through various segments including a recently added wine trading business. Following the acquisition of Fujian Laojiu Investment Co., Ltd. in May 2024, wine trading has become a reportable and operating segment, with a focus on leveraging traditional Fujian liquor brands and expanding into the broader food and beverage market.

An announcement from Sunway International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0058) ) is now available.

Sunway International Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 5 June 2026 in Hong Kong, with contingency arrangements for severe weather, to present the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended 31 December 2025. Shareholders will vote on the re-election of one executive director and one independent non-executive director, authorise the board to set directors’ pay, re-appoint the auditor, and grant the board a general mandate to issue up to 20 per cent of the company’s existing share capital, a move that could provide financing flexibility but also implies potential dilution for existing investors.

The proposed share issuance mandate, which excludes rights issues, share option exercises, scrip dividends and conversions under existing instruments, would run until the next annual meeting or earlier termination, aligning Sunway with common Hong Kong corporate governance practice. These resolutions collectively aim to refresh the company’s governance framework and capital management flexibility for the coming year, signalling a routine but important reset of board authority and capital-raising options that will shape how Sunway can respond to market opportunities and funding needs.

More about Sunway International Holdings Limited

Sunway International Holdings Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 58. Tang Palace (China) Holdings Limited has announced an update on a legal dispute involving its wholly owned Shenzhen restaurant subsidiary and one of its branch stores, confirming the case has advanced to an appeal stage in the mainland judicial system. The litigation centers on claims brought by Shenzhen Sen Wang Property Management Co., Ltd. against the group’s Shenzhen entity, highlighting the operational and legal exposure that comes with its on-the-ground presence in China’s catering industry.

The Shenzhen Intermediate People’s Court has dismissed the plaintiff’s appeal and upheld the original Futian Court ruling in favor of Tang Palace’s subsidiary, making the judgment final and conclusive. Sichuan Kelun-Biotech Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6990) ) has issued an update.

Sichuan Kelun-Biotech Biopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. has completed the full circulation conversion of 8,214,278 domestic and unlisted foreign shares into H shares, representing about 3.52% of its total share capital. The converted H shares will begin trading on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on April 20, 2026, involving three participating shareholders, including Wealthy Linkage Limited, Future Industry Investment Fund Phase II and Leyue Capital Limited, thereby modestly increasing the company’s freely tradable H share float and enhancing its market liquidity.

This move is expected to strengthen the company’s presence in Hong Kong’s capital market by expanding the portion of shares available to international investors. The completion of the H share full circulation marks a further step in aligning the company’s share structure with market practices for cross-border listed Chinese biopharmaceutical firms, which may improve trading efficiency and broaden its investor base over time.

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co ( (HK:2238) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co. has scheduled a board meeting for 29 April 2026 to review and approve its unaudited first-quarter results for the period ended 31 March 2026, prepared under PRC Generally Accepted Accounting Principles. The announcement signals the upcoming disclosure of the group’s early 2026 operating performance, a key data point for investors tracking the company’s financial health and market trajectory.

The board, led by chairman Feng Xingya and comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, will consider these figures among other matters at the meeting. The planned review underscores the company’s adherence to regular financial reporting obligations in Hong Kong and provides a timetable for stakeholders awaiting insight into the automaker’s quarterly results.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2238) stock is a Buy with a HK$4.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Guangzhou Automobile Group Co stock, see the HK:2238 Stock Forecast page.

Guangzhou Automobile Group Co., Ltd. is a Chinese automotive manufacturer based in Guangzhou, focusing on the production and sale of vehicles and related automotive services. The company is listed in Hong Kong under stock code 2238 and operates through various subsidiaries across the automotive value chain in the People’s Republic of China.

data.title: Hans Group Warns Soaring Diesel Costs May Hit Earnings Despite Hong Kong Relief Measures
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Hans Energy Co. Ltd. ( (HK:0554) ).

Hans Group Holdings Limited has warned that the sharp rise in diesel prices triggered by conflict in the Middle East is significantly increasing its operating costs, with diesel reportedly more than doubling from about US$90 to over US$200 per barrel. As the Group’s businesses are sensitive to fuel costs, this surge in energy prices creates heightened volatility and risks for its financial performance.

The Hong Kong government has introduced short‑term support measures, including temporary diesel subsidies for public and commercial vehicles and a 50% cut in tunnel tolls for commercial traffic, which will only partially offset the Group’s higher fuel expenses. Hans Group plans to closely monitor market developments and work with authorities on operational and financial responses, but it cautions shareholders and potential investors that elevated fuel prices may materially impact future results.

More about Hans Energy Co. Ltd.

Hans Group Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong‑listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, operating through a group structure that is exposed to diesel fuel costs, particularly in relation to public and commercial transport activities. The Group’s performance is closely tied to fuel price trends and the broader energy cost environment affecting Hong Kong’s transport and logistics sectors.

Average Trading Volume: 672,400

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$861.4M
